_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies RI as 'photo ID requested' (not required). Rhode Island requests but does not require photo ID; voters without an acceptable photo ID may cast a regular ballot after providing their name, address, and signature. Rhode Island enacted no-excuse absentee voting in 2021. 2026 primary date (Sept 8) follows standard RI schedule; TODO verify for 2026 cycle. Registration deadline is 30 days before the election per RI law.

earlyVoting.notes: In-person early voting available. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
In-person early voting at local boards of canvassers. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
Rhode Island offers in-person early voting at local boards of canvassers. No-excuse absentee mail voting is also available. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

acceptedIds: Rhode Island driver's license or state ID
Student ID from a Rhode Island school
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Rhode Island does not have a statewide prohibition on phones in polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot or other voters. Electioneering is prohibited within 50 feet of a polling place entrance.
additionalRules: Photo ID is requested but not required. Voters without an acceptable photo ID may sign the poll book and vote a regular ballot after providing their name and address.
Semi-closed primary: registered party members vote in their party's primary. Unaffiliated voters may not vote in partisan primaries.
No-excuse absentee voting is available for all registered voters.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request a mail ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee voting, enacted 2021)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 4 days before the election (received by local board of canvassers); online/in-person applications accepted until 4 days before
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the local board of canvassers by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the board of canvassers by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
